name: agentlink
description: MCP server that provides AI agents with native VS Code tools — diff-based file editing, integrated terminal, diagnostics

commands.title: AgentLink: Set OpenAI API Key for Embeddings
AgentLink: Re-sign In / Replace ChatGPT/Codex Account

agentlink.port.description: HTTP port for the MCP server. Use 0 (default) for an OS-assigned port — required for multi-window support. Set a fixed port only if you need a stable URL.

agentlink.browserGatewayPort.description: Stable localhost port for the shared AgentLink browser gateway helper. Use a fixed value so the remote UI URL is bookmarkable.

agentlink.browserGatewayLanAccess.description: Expose the browser gateway on your LAN so phones and other devices can connect (e.g. http://agentlink.local:<port>). Requires per-device pairing (run /pair in the chat, then enter the code on the new device). When disabled the gateway only accepts loopback connections. AgentLink will restart the shared gateway helper automatically the next time it's used after this setting changes.

agentlink.browserGatewayMdnsName.description: Hostname to advertise over mDNS when LAN access is enabled (resolves as <name>.local on the same network). If the name collides on the network, a short suffix is appended automatically.

agentlink.autoStart.description: Automatically start the MCP server on extension activation

agentlink.masterBypass.description: Skip ALL approval prompts (file writes and commands auto-approved). Use with caution.

agentlink.diagnosticDelay.description: Max milliseconds to wait after file save for diagnostics to settle

agentlink.agents.description: Which AI agents to auto-configure MCP for. Leave empty to use only the built-in AgentLink agent. Each selected agent's config file is written on server start and cleaned up on stop.

agentlink.autoUpdateInstructions.description: Automatically update agent instruction files on startup. Enabled when you set up instructions during onboarding.

agentlink.autoUpdateHooks.description: Automatically update PreToolUse hooks on startup. Enabled when you install hooks during onboarding.

agentlink.requireAuth.description: Require auth token on the MCP endpoint (recommended)

agentlink.semanticSearchEnabled.description: Enable semantic codebase search via Qdrant vector index. Requires Qdrant running and an OpenAI API key for embeddings.

agentlink.qdrantUrl.description: Qdrant vector database URL for semantic codebase search

agentlink.openaiCompatible.baseUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL used by helper-model features (question detection, background-agent summaries). Examples: LM Studio http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1, OpenAI https://api.openai.com/v1, OpenRouter https://openrouter.ai/api/v1.

agentlink.openaiCompatible.model.description: Model id for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Leave empty to let the server pick (LM Studio uses the loaded model).

agentlink.openaiCompatible.apiKey.description: Optional API key sent as Bearer token. LM Studio ignores it; hosted endpoints (OpenAI, OpenRouter, etc.) require it.

agentlink.openaiCompatible.timeoutMs.description: Max milliseconds to wait for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint before falling back.

enumDescriptions: Use the built-in regex heuristic (no LLM call).
Use the current chat provider's low-tier model (Claude Haiku, gpt-5.4-mini, etc.). Spends your provider quota on every idle turn.
Use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint configured under agentlink.openaiCompatible.*.
agentlink.questionDetection.mode.description: How to detect whether the agent is asking a question before it idles, and generate answer buttons.

enumDescriptions: Use the background session's own provider (Haiku / gpt-5.4-mini family).
Use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint configured under agentlink.openaiCompatible.*.
Skip the LLM call and show only the inferred phase status.
agentlink.bgSummary.mode.description: How the tiny status string shown for background agents is summarized.

agentlink.questionDetection.llmEnabled.description: Deprecated: use agentlink.questionDetection.mode instead. When true, mapped to mode="openai".

agentlink.autoIndex.description: Automatically index the codebase on startup when semantic search is enabled.

enumDescriptions: Function/method-level chunks. Efficient with good recall.
Adds statement-level chunks for finer-grained search. ~50% more embeddings.

agentlink.indexExclusions.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from codebase indexing (in addition to .gitignore).

enumDescriptions: Opens an editor tab to the right of the active editor (split view). Closes when all approvals are handled.
Shows a tab in the bottom panel alongside Terminal/Problems. Can be dragged to the secondary sidebar.

agentlink.recentApprovalTtl.description: Seconds to remember a single-use command approval (run-once / accept). Repeat identical commands within this window are auto-approved. Set to 0 to disable.

enumDescriptions: Every command not covered by an explicit command rule prompts for approval (current behavior).
Auto-approve read-only commands (ls, git status, rg, version checks). Anything that mutates state still prompts.

Also auto-approve workspace-local mutations, project toolchain commands (npm install/test, builds), and unrecognized plain commands. This trusts your project's scripts and tooling to run without per-command review. Destructive, external, privileged, or obfuscated commands always prompt.
agentlink.commandAutoApproveTier.description: Auto-approve agent terminal commands classified at or below this safety tier. Dangerous commands (rm, sudo, git push, network egress, writes outside the workspace, inline interpreter code) always require approval regardless of this setting. Explicit command rules, recent single-use approvals, and masterBypass take precedence.

agentlink.worktreeDirectorySuffix.description: Suffix for the sibling worktree container used by start_worktree_agent when no worktreePath is provided. Default paths are <repo-parent>/<repo-name><suffix>/<sanitized-workstream-name>.

agentlink.agentModel.description: Legacy global fallback model for the built-in agent chat. Use agentlink.modeModelPreferences for mode-specific defaults.

agentlink.modeModelPreferences.description: Default model per chat mode, keyed by mode slug (e.g. code, architect, ask, debug, review). Manually selecting a model in a mode updates that mode's saved preference.

agentlink.agentMaxTokens.description: Maximum output tokens per agent response.

agentlink.thinkingBudget.description: Token budget for extended thinking (only used with thinking-capable models like claude-opus-4-8 and claude-sonnet-4-6).

agentlink.showThinking.description: Show thinking blocks in the agent chat UI.

agentlink.anthropic.dynamicModelCapabili.description: Fetch Anthropic model list and capabilities (context window, output tokens, reasoning efforts) from the Anthropic API and merge them over the built-in defaults. Refreshes lazily (never on activation) and falls back to the built-in static metadata when offline or the API is unavailable. Disable to use only the built-in static metadata.

agentlink.autoCondense.description: Automatically condense the conversation context when the context window fills up.

agentlink.autoCondenseThreshold.description: Legacy global auto-condense threshold used for migration. New per-model thresholds are stored in agentlink.modelCondenseThresholds.

agentlink.modelCondenseThresholds.description: Per-model auto-condense thresholds keyed by model ID. Models with 1M+ context windows default to 0.7; other models default to 0.9 when no override is stored.

agentlink.writeRules.description: Glob patterns for files that are always auto-approved for writes without showing a diff view. Supports workspace-relative paths (e.g. "**/*.test.ts") and absolute paths (e.g. "/tmp/**").

agentlink.defaultMode.description: Default mode for new agent chat sessions.

agentlink.codexStatefulResponses.description: Enable OpenAI Responses chaining for Codex/API-key sessions using previous_response_id when available. When a prior response cannot be resumed, AgentLink falls back to full local replay for that turn.

agentlink.codexStoreResponses.description: When Codex stateful responses are enabled, also set store=true on OpenAI Responses requests so server-side response state can be retained. Disabled by default to preserve existing retention behavior.

scripts.lint: bash -c 'e=0; tsc --noEmit || e=1; tsc -p src/agent/webview/tsconfig.json --noEmit || e=1; tsc -p src/sidebar/webview/tsconfig.json --noEmit || e=1; tsc -p src/approvals/webview/tsconfig.json --noEmit || e=1; tsc -p src/browser-gateway/webview/tsconfig.json --noEmit || e=1; oxlint src/ --deny-warnings || e=1; exit $e'
